gc_controller_deployment.note: Deployment accepted, ReplicaSet created, toleration patch applied, taint removed. After RAM increase and reboot, pods reached ContainerCreating state but control-plane crashed before they completed startup.

runtime_reconciliation.blocker: Control-plane instability persists across all resource tiers (4 GB→6 GB→10 GB, 2→4 vCPUs, fresh kubeadm reinstall). Fourth attempt with 4 vCPUs + 10 GB + fresh kubeadm init gave ~8 min of stability before identical failure: API server etcd timeout → CM/scheduler lease loss → CrashLoopBackOff. Root cause is definitively NOT memory or CPU starvation (7.7 GB available, no OOM, 4 vCPUs idle). Suspected: k3s server process interference, containerd 1.7.24 + kernel 6.12.74 incompatibility, or kubeadm v1.36.2 static pod behavior on Debian 13.

vm_retry_history.result: CP unstable, CRD/CRUD/negative/RBAC pass during API up windows
CP initially stable after reboot, controller pods reached ContainerCreating. CP crashed 10+ min into runtime validation (CM: lease loss, Scheduler: lease loss). No OOM.

vm_retry_history.action: RAM increased to 10 GB (cold boot, setmaxmem)
vm_retry_history.result: CP unstable with same failure pattern: API server etcd timeout, CM/scheduler lease loss. 7.7 GB available, no OOM. k3s server process detected (6% MEM). 2 vCPUs. Root cause not memory-related.

Fresh kubeadm init succeeded, CP was stable for ~8 min after init (GCP CRD installed, test pods scheduled, controller binary uploaded). CP then degraded into API server etcd timeout then CrashLoopBackOff. Identical failure pattern. Root cause is resolved to be NOT memory or CPU — likely k3s interference, containerd 1.7.24 + kernel 6.12.74 incompatibility, or kubeadm v1.36.2 static pod behavior on Debian 13.

known_issues: Control-plane instability persists across ALL resource tiers (4/6/10 GB RAM, 2/4 vCPUs, fresh reinstall). CM/scheduler exit with code 1 (Error) due to leader election lease loss when API server becomes unreachable.
GC controller cannot run on this VM — CP remains unstable regardless of resources or reinstall.
Root cause definitively NOT memory or CPU (7.7 GB available, 4 vCPUs idle, no OOM). 4th retry with fresh kubeadm init gave ~8 min stability then identical failure.
Host has a running k3s server process (6% MEM, ~12% CPU) — likely interferes with kubeadm's etcd or API server.
kube-proxy in CrashLoopBackOff (known flannel/kube-proxy interaction may compound instability).
v1.36 kind and k3d PASS results demonstrate runtime behavior on stable clusters (see kind.md, k3d.md).
limitations: CRD/API compatibility, CRUD lifecycle, negative schema, and RBAC boundaries validated (PASS during API up windows).
Runtime controller reconciliation BLOCKED — control-plane instability persists after RAM increase and reboot.
Only tested on single-node control-plane with flannel CNI.
v1.36 kind and k3d evidence exists for runtime behavior (see kind.md, k3d.md).
